summary.title: Summary
highlights: Six PRs merged this period, with the composable multi-agent architecture landing as the major milestone — adding four named agents (intake, refine, knock-out-todos, scout), 22 reusable task files, a plugin system, and a redesigned worker entrypoint.

Developer experience improved significantly: /what-now now serves as the single entry point with an urgency-ranked menu, /pr-review codifies PR standards, and all lib commands are hidden from the IDE palette.
CI/CD was streamlined by extracting the GitHub Pages build into a reusable workflow in NoahWright87/actions, making the pattern available to any downstream repo.
Two PRs are currently in review — one extending the agent fleet with a new Bard agent, and one completing the composable architecture with task manifests and fleet manager.

themes.title: Composable Agent Architecture
themes.description: Multi-agent worker infrastructure with per-agent task files, plugin system, and redesigned entrypoint.

items.text: 4 named agents with dedicated instruction files (intake, refine, knock-out-todos, scout)
22 reusable task files in agents/tasks/ shared across agents
Plugin system via .claude-plugin/ for distribution with `claude plugin install`
Worker entrypoint redesigned: per-agent check/startup scripts, situation reports, config v2, GitHub App auth

links.label: Back-merge changes from work
links.description: Ports evolved composable agents/ directory, plugin system, and worker upgrades from internal fork.

links.label: Port multi-agent worker infrastructure from internal fork
links.description: Replaces monolithic worker-instructions.md with multi-agent dispatch; adds Docker Compose and Kubernetes manifests.

themes.title: Commands & Developer Experience
themes.description: /what-now as a single ranked entry point, /pr-review command, hidden lib commands.

items.text: /what-now runs a pre-flight check and labels options ⭐ Highly recommended / Recommended by urgency
/pr-review codifies PR expectations: self-review, batch fixes, explanatory comments, reviewer replies
knock-out-todos now opens PRs automatically and chains into /pr-review
All lib commands hidden from IDE palette — /what-now is the only visible entry point

links.label: Add /what-now status assessment and /pr-review command
links.description: Urgency-ranked /what-now menu; /pr-review codifies the full PR lifecycle for agents.

links.label: Add /what-now entry point, hide lib commands, restructure PHILOSOPHY.md
links.description: Moves individual commands into lib/ so only /what-now appears in the IDE; restructures PHILOSOPHY.md around AI-adapted best practices.

themes.title: CI/CD & Documentation
themes.description: Reusable Pages workflow, README cleanup, stale file removal.

items.text: pages.yml extracted into reusable workflow at NoahWright87/actions — any repo can now publish docs with 8 lines of YAML
Reusable workflow generates a Primer CSS landing page listing all published folders
README updated with /what-now, /refine, /pr-review in What You Get table; stale root-level refine.md removed

links.label: Migrate pages.yml to reusable workflow in NoahWright87/actions
links.description: Extracts GitHub Pages build into a parameterized reusable workflow for downstream reuse.

links.label: Fix READMEs and remove stale root-level refine.md
links.description: Updates command tables and flow diagram; removes leftover duplicate file.
